note: MLETR jurisdiction-adoption static lookup (MC-3). Vector 1: both corridor legs MLETR-adopted. Vector 2: mixed adopted/aligned corridor. Vector 3: unknown jurisdiction, finite gap-verdict edge case.

vectors.name: corridor_both_adopted
vectors.description: UK -> Singapore: both jurisdictions have enacted MLETR-based legislation.

origin.name: United Kingdom
origin.scope: Possessable electronic documents incl. bills of lading, bills of exchange, promissory notes.

destination.name: Singapore
destination.scope: Electronic transferable records -- first national MLETR adoption worldwide.
destination.citation: Singapore Statutes, Electronic Transactions Act Part IVA

output_payload.disambiguation: lookup_mletr_jurisdiction_adoption is a static citation-table lookup -- statute, scope, and effective date per jurisdiction, plus a corridor verdict. It is NOT a legal opinion and does NOT assess a specific eBL document (use validate_mletr_record, art-53, for a document-level MLETR Art.10/11 conformance check).
output_payload.table_source: UNCITRAL Model Law on Electronic Transferable Records (MLETR, 2017) national-adoption tracking (uncitral.org/en/texts/ecommerce/modellaw/electronic_transferable_records/status); national gazette/statute citations per jurisdiction, verified at build.

vectors.name: corridor_mixed_adopted_aligned
vectors.description: United States -> United Kingdom: origin is 'aligned' (UCC Art.12, not literal MLETR), destination is 'adopted'.

origin.name: United States
origin.statute: Uniform Commercial Code Article 12 -- Controllable Electronic Records (2022 UCC Amendments), state-by-state enactment
origin.scope: Controllable-electronic-records framework -- functionally aligned, not a literal MLETR enactment. Adoption is state-by-state; confirm the specific state.
origin.citation: Uniform Law Commission, 2022 UCC Amendments, UCC Art. 12

destination.name: United Kingdom
destination.scope: Possessable electronic documents incl. bills of lading, bills of exchange, promissory notes.

output_payload.disambiguation: lookup_mletr_jurisdiction_adoption is a static citation-table lookup -- statute, scope, and effective date per jurisdiction, plus a corridor verdict. It is NOT a legal opinion and does NOT assess a specific eBL document (use validate_mletr_record, art-53, for a document-level MLETR Art.10/11 conformance check).
output_payload.table_source: UNCITRAL Model Law on Electronic Transferable Records (MLETR, 2017) national-adoption tracking (uncitral.org/en/texts/ecommerce/modellaw/electronic_transferable_records/status); national gazette/statute citations per jurisdiction, verified at build.

vectors.name: corridor_unknown_jurisdiction_finite
vectors.description: France -> unrecognised jurisdiction string: gap verdict, finite (no NaN/Infinity), demonstrates the not-adopted fallback.

origin.name: France
origin.statute: Decree No. 2025-811 (national transposition of the EU electronic transferable-records digitalisation framework)
origin.scope: First EU Member State full MLETR-aligned transposition.

destination.name: Ruritania
destination.scope: No MLETR-aligned electronic transferable-record legislation identified as of MLETR-JURISDICTION-ADOPTION-2026-07-17-V1.

output_payload.disambiguation: lookup_mletr_jurisdiction_adoption is a static citation-table lookup -- statute, scope, and effective date per jurisdiction, plus a corridor verdict. It is NOT a legal opinion and does NOT assess a specific eBL document (use validate_mletr_record, art-53, for a document-level MLETR Art.10/11 conformance check).
